_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, scorecard-refine-local cron)
_meta.note: FL local batch (3) — Cocoa Council D2 Deputy Mayor Hearn (D, sitting since 2020) + Cocoa Council D4 Koss (D, sitting since 2018, re-elected Nov 2022) + Melbourne Council D1 Smith (nonpartisan, sitting since Nov 2024, term to Nov 2028). Rosters web-verified June 2026 against city sites. Every TRUE/FALSE cites a primary or reputable source; null where no evidence (no party-default inference). Public_justice q0 held to the AFFIRMATIVE advocacy bar — Hearn (firefighter/EMT career) / Koss / Smith (NNO planning committee) all NULL on that cell per skill discipline. INCIDENT NOTE: This batch had originally included rick-west (VA Chesapeake Mayor) and andre-dickens (Atlanta Mayor GA), but POST-PUSH AUDIT revealed both refinements landed on the WRONG records due to slug-collisions (rick-west polluted OK State Rep IDX 5439; andre-dickens overwrote already-evidence_local GA record IDX 8955 while FL ghost IDX 7663 remained unrefined). The polluting commit (80ae9f18909) was REVERTED via 22469a8501f and BOTH slug-collisions flagged for manual dedup-merge before next refinement attempt. Same incident shape as batch 25 (rick-west slug collision); prior auto-defer discipline was right.

set.office: City Council, District 2 (Deputy Mayor), City of Cocoa, Florida (sitting since Nov 2020 · Deputy Mayor since Nov 2021)

profile.background: Lavander Hearn — Cocoa High School alum; Eastern Florida State College + University of Florida; AS Fire Science, BBA, MS Emergency Management/Disaster Mitigation. Brevard County Fire Rescue Firefighter/EMT; substitute teacher Brevard Public Schools; currently works in Emergency Management field. As a teen had 'incidents involving the law' and was turned around via youth mentorship — informs his youth-outreach focus. Cocoa City Council District 2 since Nov 2020; selected Deputy Mayor unanimously by Council Nov 2021. Appointed to Florida League of Cities Young Elected Municipal Officials Advisory Council. Brevard County Democratic Party rostered.

lavander-hearn.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting District 2 Councilman / Deputy Mayor of Cocoa as of June 2026 (cocoafl.gov bio live; 2024 reelection cycle timing consistent with continuing term through ~2028). Confidence held at roster_verified — exhaustive 10-min sweep of cocoafl.gov, Cocoa Council minutes, Florida Today, ClickOrlando, Spectrum News 13, Space Coast Daily, Brevard County Democratic Party site, and his official Facebook page surfaced ONE documented policy action (May 2022 rent-notice ordinance, a tenant-protection measure that doesn't map to any of the 30 LOCAL-rubric cells) and no statements on any culture-war / fiscal / criminal-justice / immigration / 2A / election cell. All 30 cells null — honest, not lazy. First-responder career is first-responder-allied but, per skill discipline, does NOT qualify as affirmative public 'back the blue' advocacy for public_justice q0. Documented policy footprint: rent-notice ordinance only.

set.office: City Council, District 4, City of Cocoa, Florida (sitting since 2018 · re-elected Nov 2022 · term through Nov 2026)

profile.background: Lorraine Koss — Retired Deputy Director, U.S. Water Alliance. Master's in Environmental Management, Duke University; Bachelor's in Communications, Trinity University. 30+ years water-resources policy. Founded Cocoa Sustainability Advisory Committee; chaired Utility Advisory Board; first chair and continuing member of the Save Our Indian River Lagoon Citizen Oversight Committee (steering $300M, 10-year lagoon plan); organized FL SUN Space Coast Solar Co-op. Serves on Space Coast EDC and Space Coast Transportation Planning Organization Executive Committee. Appointed to Florida League of Cities 2024-2025 Utilities & Natural Resources Committee. Cocoa City Council District 4 since 2018; re-elected November 2022. Married to Lawrence Koss; two sons (each served five tours in Iraq); one grandson. Self-describes in 2022 as 'an independent voice'; listed as Democratic elected official on BrevardDems site.

lorraine-koss.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting District 4 Councilwoman of Cocoa as of June 2026 (cocoafl.gov bio + Wikipedia city article confirm). Profile is a sustainability/water/stormwater specialist — heavy regional-board entanglement (FL League of Cities, EDC, TPO, Save Our Indian River Lagoon committee). HARD evidence cited: self_defense q1 FALSE — per profile, 'worked with Ohioans for Gun Safety on a ballot initiative for universal background checks on gun sales' — documented pre-Cocoa-Council active support for expanded gun-control via background-check expansion, contrary to rubric's opposition to registries/universal-check mandates. All other 29 cells null after sweep of cocoafl.gov, Council minutes, Florida Today, ClickOrlando, Spectrum News 13, Space Coast Daily, BrevardDems site, LinkedIn (fetch-blocked), and campaign Facebook. No public faith profession, no abortion/marriage/sexuality/school-policy/immigration/police statements located.

1.note: Profile documents pre-Cocoa-Council active support for expanded gun control: 'worked with Ohioans for Gun Safety on a ballot initiative for universal background checks on gun sales' — contrary to rubric's opposition to registries / universal-check mandates

set.office: City Council, District 1, City of Melbourne, Florida (sitting since Nov 2024 · term to Nov 2028)

profile.background: Marcus Smith — Retired U.S. Air Force Lt. Col., ~30 yrs combined service (Navy enlisted → AF officer, retired 2021). B.S. Electrical Engineering Georgia Tech; MBA Webster University. Financial representative at Sky Peak Financial; owner Tax Time Solutions LLC since 2005. Chaired Melbourne Planning & Zoning 2021–2024. American Legion Post 191 1st Vice. Kappa Alpha Psi alumnus; Mason (Lodge #318). First elected Nov 5, 2024 with ~52% in nonpartisan general; term to Nov 2028. Serves on Melbourne National Night Out planning committee since 2020. Endorsed by Krimson PAC.

marcus-smith.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting District 1 Council Member of Melbourne as of June 2026 (term to Nov 2028 per melbourneflorida.org). Nonpartisan seat but documented voting record reads as center-left independent — NOT the assumed 'retired military = likely conservative' prior. HARD evidence cited: economic_stewardship q2 FALSE — Sept 23/24, 2025 voted AYE to adopt $315.4M FY2025-26 budget and set operating millage at 7.0112 mills, an 11.4% INCREASE over the rollback rate (functionally a tax hike on residents). All other 29 cells null after sweep of melbourneflorida.org Council minutes, Florida Today, Space Coast Daily, Fox 35 Orlando, Spectrum News 13, FL Voice News, his campaign site, and Brevard GOP/Dem sites. DEVIATION FLAGS (not cell-scored): (a) Sept 24, 2025 voted NO on Mayor Alfrey's proposal to rename Cypress Ave 'Charlie Kirk Lane' after Kirk's assassination, with stated rationale 'many of his statements were divisive' — per prior Melbourne-batch Kirk Lane discipline this leaves christian_liberty NULL because the rationale was 'divisive' not explicitly anti-Christian; (b) endorsed by Krimson PAC (self-described progressive PAC). National Night Out planning-committee service is community engagement, NOT affirmative public 'back the blue' advocacy — public_justice q0 NULL per skill discipline.

2.note: Sept 23/24, 2025 voted AYE to adopt $315.4M FY2025-26 budget and set operating millage at 7.0112 mills, an 11.4% INCREASE over the rollback rate — functionally a tax increase on residents, contrary to fiscal-restraint posture
